findings.title: Dead import: 'classifyTask' imported but never called in factory.ts
findings.detail: src/pipeline/factory.ts line 11 imports both 'classifyTask' and 'modelToTier'. 'modelToTier' is used at line ~371. 'classifyTask' is never called — routing now goes through routingStrategy.classify() in default-routing-strategy.ts. ESLint confirms the error: '@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars'.
findings.fix_sketch: Change line 11 to: import { modelToTier } from '../classifier/index.js';

findings.title: markCapabilityBlocked omits LABEL_IFLEET_IN_PROGRESS removal — label stuck permanently
findings.detail: markCapabilityBlocked removes LABEL_IN_FLIGHT but omits LABEL_IFLEET_IN_PROGRESS, leaving 'ifleet:in_progress' permanently stuck on blocked issues. markCompleted and markFailed both correctly remove both labels. The asymmetry makes GitHub's UI show blocked issues as still in-progress.
findings.fix_sketch: Add: await this.removeLabel(task, LABEL_IFLEET_IN_PROGRESS); after the existing removeLabel(task, LABEL_IN_FLIGHT) call in markCapabilityBlocked.

findings.title: build-cards.ts uses (store as any).db instead of public store.getDb() API
findings.detail: src/learning/reviewer-prefs/build-cards.ts line ~184 casts to 'as any' to access the private 'db' field. TaskStore.getDb() is the public API for exactly this purpose. The cast bypasses TypeScript and silently breaks if the private field name changes.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace (store as any).db with store.getDb(). Zero behavior change.

findings.title: Number() without NaN guard for CONTROL_PLANE_PORT and IFLEET_DAEMON_TICK_MS env vars
findings.detail: Number(process.env['CONTROL_PLANE_PORT'] ?? DEFAULT_DAEMON_PORT) returns NaN for non-numeric strings. NaN as a port causes a cryptic bind error. NaN as tick interval makes setInterval(fn, NaN) which Node.js coerces to 0 — a tight spin loop that saturates one CPU core. Same pattern for IFLEET_DAEMON_TICK_MS.
findings.fix_sketch: After each Number() call add: if (!Number.isFinite(port) || port < 1 || port > 65535) throw new Error('CONTROL_PLANE_PORT must be a valid port number'); and if (!Number.isFinite(tickIntervalMs) || tickIntervalMs < 1) throw new Error('IFLEET_DAEMON_TICK_MS must be a positive integer');

findings.title: git-add failure in commitEditorChanges silently swallowed — downstream reports misleading 'no diff' error
findings.detail: When 'git add -A' fails (locked index, permission error), commitEditorChanges logs a warning and returns early. runEditor then calls git.diff() which returns empty. The runner reports 'editor produced no diff — possible silent tool-use failure', misattributing a git-add failure as a Claude tool-use failure.
findings.fix_sketch: Upgraded console.warn to console.error with a clearer message explaining downstream diff will be empty. Full throw-and-propagate would require updating tests that rely on silent failure with fake worktree paths.

findings.title: rowToTask corrupt source_data fallback { kind: 'unknown' } is an invalid TaskSource cast
findings.detail: store.ts rowToTask falls back to { kind: 'unknown' } for corrupt source_data and casts to QueuedTask['source']. 'unknown' is not a valid TaskSource discriminant. When mark* methods encounter it they throw 'cannot mark a unknown task' — confusing rather than the original parse error. Root cause is already logged by safeJsonParse.
findings.fix_sketch: Throw from rowToTask when source_data fails to parse, with a clear '[store] task <id> has corrupt source_data' message. Callers that receive null already handle it; the rows.map(rowToTask) call site needs filtering added.

findings.title: onApprove/onVerify/onForcePr return 202 success on public port when commands cannot be fulfilled
findings.detail: server.ts public port handlers for onApprove, onVerify, onForcePr log console.error and return normally, causing the control plane to send a 202 response. Discord operators see a false-positive success with no user-facing feedback. The comment in code acknowledges this as an architectural limitation (throw after res.json(202) creates unhandled exception).
findings.fix_sketch: Requires refactoring the control plane to defer sending the response until after the handler runs. Architectural change — deferred to backlog. Current mitigation: console.error log is already present.

findings.title: accumulateCost falsy guard early-returns for costUsd === 0
findings.detail: if (!costUsd) returns early for both undefined (intended) and 0 (unintended). A genuinely $0 call skips the saveSprintSpend DB persist. Mathematically correct but semantically wrong — NaN also falsy and silently treated the same.
findings.fix_sketch: Change to: if (costUsd == null || !Number.isFinite(costUsd)) return ...; to explicitly guard undefined/null/NaN while allowing 0.

findings.title: PR number parse failure in buildPrOpener silently skips reviewer assignment with no log entry
findings.detail: If gh's stdout format changes so the URL no longer ends with '/<number>', match is null, number defaults to 0, and reviewer assignment is silently dropped with no log trace.
findings.fix_sketch: Add: if (number === 0) console.warn('[pr] could not parse PR number from URL:', url, '— reviewer assignment skipped');

findings.title: watchForNew 'seen' set grows unboundedly over daemon lifetime
findings.detail: The 'seen' Set in watchForNew accumulates every GitHub issue node_id ever observed and is never pruned. Slow monotonic memory growth bounded by total GitHub issues across watched repos (likely thousands — unlikely to OOM but is a resource leak).
findings.fix_sketch: Track a timestamp per ID and evict entries older than a TTL (e.g., 7 days) on each tick, or use a size-bounded LRU map.

findings.title: Two separate Octokit instances created from the same token in daemon.ts
findings.detail: daemon.ts creates a ThrottledOctokit for GitHubQueue and a plain Octokit for makeProductionFactory from the same token. They have separate throttle contexts so rate-limit state from queue operations is not visible to the factory's PR-open calls.
findings.fix_sketch: Instantiate one ThrottledOctokit at the top of main() and pass it to both GitHubQueue and makeProductionFactory.
